steps.title: Identify the angle relationship between x and 110°
steps.content:
Line $c$ is a transversal crossing two parallel lines $s$ and $t$.

The angle marked $110°$ is below line $t$ on the left side of the transversal, and the angle marked $x°$ is above line $s$ on the right side of the transversal.

These are co-interior angles (also called consecutive interior angles or same-side interior angles)—they're both between the parallel lines and on the same side of the transversal.

steps.title: Apply the parallel lines angle property
steps.content:
When a transversal intersects two parallel lines, co-interior angles are supplementary—they sum to $180°$.
$$x + 110 = 180$$
$$x = 70$$
The answer is 70.
